root.description: Specifies resource ARNs that are enabled for `var.iam_permissions`. Wildcards are acceptable.
root.name: iam_allowed_resources

root.description:
ID element. Usually the component or solution name, e.g. 'app' or 'jenkins'.
This is the only ID element not also included as a `tag`.
The "name" tag is set to the full `id` string. There is no tag with the value of the `name` input.
root.name: name

root.description: Route53 parent zone ID. If provided (not empty), the module will create Route53 DNS records used for verification
root.name: zone_id

root.description: If provided the module will create an SPF record for `domain`.
root.name: create_spf_record

root.description: Specifies permissions for the IAM user.
root.name: iam_permissions

root.description:
ID element. Additional attributes (e.g. `workers` or `cluster`) to add to `id`,
in the order they appear in the list. New attributes are appended to the
end of the list. The elements of the list are joined by the `delimiter`
and treated as a single ID element.
root.name: attributes

root.description:
Additional key-value pairs to add to each map in `tags_as_list_of_maps`. Not added to `tags` or `id`.
This is for some rare cases where resources want additional configuration of tags
and therefore take a list of maps with tag key, value, and additional configuration.
root.name: additional_tag_map

root.description: If provided the module will create Route53 DNS records used for DKIM verification.
root.name: verify_dkim

root.description: Create an AWS access key which is stored in the TF state (not recommended)
root.name: iam_create_access_key

root.description: ID element _(Rarely used, not included by default)_. A customer identifier, indicating who this instance of a resource is for
root.name: tenant

root.description:
Set of labels (ID elements) to include as tags in the `tags` output.
Default is to include all labels.
Tags with empty values will not be included in the `tags` output.
Set to `[]` to suppress all generated tags.
**Notes:**
  The value of the `name` tag, if included, will be the `id`, not the `name`.
  Unlike other `null-label` inputs, the initial setting of `labels_as_tags` cannot be
  changed in later chained modules. Attempts to change it will be silently ignored.
root.name: labels_as_tags

root.description:
The order in which the labels (ID elements) appear in the `id`.
Defaults to ["namespace", "environment", "stage", "name", "attributes"].
You can omit any of the 6 labels ("tenant" is the 6th), but at least one must be present.
root.name: label_order

root.description:
Controls the letter case of the `tags` keys (label names) for tags generated by this module.
Does not affect keys of tags passed in via the `tags` input.
Possible values: `lower`, `title`, `upper`.
Default value: `title`.
root.name: label_key_case

root.description: Creates a group with permission to send emails from SES domain
root.name: ses_group_enabled

root.description: Set to false to prevent the module from creating any resources
root.name: enabled

root.description:
Describe additional descriptors to be output in the `descriptors` output map.
Map of maps. Keys are names of descriptors. Values are maps of the form
`{
   format = string
   labels = list(string)
}`
(Type is `any` so the map values can later be enhanced to provide additional options.)
`format` is a Terraform format string to be passed to the `format()` function.
`labels` is a list of labels, in order, to pass to `format()` function.
Label values will be normalized before being passed to `format()` so they will be
identical to how they appear in `id`.
Default is `{}` (`descriptors` output will be empty).
root.name: descriptor_formats

root.description: The behaviour of the custom_from_subdomain when the MX record is not found. Defaults to `UseDefaultValue`.
root.name: custom_from_behavior_on_mx_failure

root.description: Create an AWS SES SMTP password which is stored in the TF state (not recommended)
root.name: iam_create_ses_smtp_password

root.description: The name of the IAM group to create. If empty the module will calculate name from a context (recommended).
root.name: ses_group_name

root.description: ID element. Usually used for region e.g. 'uw2', 'us-west-2', OR role 'prod', 'staging', 'dev', 'UAT'
root.name: environment

root.description:
Additional tags (e.g. `{'BusinessUnit': 'XYZ'}`).
Neither the tag keys nor the tag values will be modified by this module.
root.name: tags

root.description:
Terraform regular expression (regex) string.
Characters matching the regex will be removed from the ID elements.
If not set, `"/[^a-zA-Z0-9-]/"` is used to remove all characters other than hyphens, letters and digits.
root.name: regex_replace_chars

root.description: If enabled the module will create a Route53 DNS record for the `From` address subdomain.
root.name: custom_from_dns_record_enabled

root.description: Creates user with permission to send emails from SES domain
root.name: ses_user_enabled

root.default:
{
  "additional_tag_map": {},
  "attributes": [],
  "delimiter": null,
  "descriptor_formats": {},
  "enabled": true,
  "environment": null,
  "id_length_limit": null,
  "label_key_case": null,
  "label_order": [],
  "label_value_case": null,
  "labels_as_tags": [
    "unset"
  ],
  "name": null,
  "namespace": null,
  "regex_replace_chars": null,
  "stage": null,
  "tags": {},
  "tenant": null
}
root.description:
Single object for setting entire context at once.
See description of individual variables for details.
Leave string and numeric variables as `null` to use default value.
Individual variable settings (non-null) override settings in context object,
except for attributes, tags, and additional_tag_map, which are merged.
root.name: context

root.description:
Limit `id` to this many characters (minimum 6).
Set to `0` for unlimited length.
Set to `null` for keep the existing setting, which defaults to `0`.
Does not affect `id_full`.
root.name: id_length_limit

root.description: The domain to create the SES identity for.
root.name: domain

root.description: The IAM Path of the group to create
root.name: ses_group_path

root.description: ID element. Usually used to indicate role, e.g. 'prod', 'staging', 'source', 'build', 'test', 'deploy', 'release'
root.name: stage

root.description: If provided the module will create Route53 DNS records used for domain verification.
root.name: verify_domain

root.description: ID element. Usually an abbreviation of your organization name, e.g. 'eg' or 'cp', to help ensure generated IDs are globally unique
root.name: namespace

root.description:
Delimiter to be used between ID elements.
Defaults to `-` (hyphen). Set to `""` to use no delimiter at all.
root.name: delimiter

root.description:
Controls the letter case of ID elements (labels) as included in `id`,
set as tag values, and output by this module individually.
Does not affect values of tags passed in via the `tags` input.
Possible values: `lower`, `title`, `upper` and `none` (no transformation).
Set this to `title` and set `delimiter` to `""` to yield Pascal Case IDs.
Default value: `lower`.
root.name: label_value_case

root.description: If provided the module will create a custom subdomain for the `From` address.
root.name: custom_from_subdomain

root.description: The SPF record for the domain. This is a TXT record that should be added to the domain's DNS settings to allow SES to send emails on behalf of the domain.
root.name: spf_record

root.description: The custom mail FROM domain
root.name: custom_from_domain

root.description: Normalized IAM user name.
root.name: user_name

root.description: The ARN assigned by AWS for this user.
root.name: user_arn

root.description: The IAM group name
root.name: ses_group_name

root.description: The IAM secret for usage with SES API. This will be written to the state file in plain text.
root.name: secret_access_key

root.description: A code which when added to the domain as a TXT record will signal to SES that the owner of the domain has authorised SES to act on their behalf. The domain identity will be in state 'verification pending' until this is done. See below for an example of how this might be achieved when the domain is hosted in Route 53 and managed by Terraform. Find out more about verifying domains in Amazon SES in the AWS SES docs.
root.name: ses_domain_identity_verification_token

root.description: The unique ID assigned by AWS.
root.name: user_unique_id

root.description: The SMTP password. This will be written to the state file in plain text.
root.name: ses_smtp_password

root.description: The SMTP user which is access key ID.
root.name: access_key_id

root.description: The ARN of the SES domain identity
root.name: ses_domain_identity_arn

root.description: A list of DKIM Tokens which, when added to the DNS Domain as CNAME records, allows for receivers to verify that emails were indeed authorized by the domain owner.
root.name: ses_dkim_tokens
